source.title: Structural Damage - High Pressure Hose Rupture

context.region: Gulf of America - Garden Banks Block 215

actions_taken: Three injured personnel evaluated by Rig Medic
Two personnel evacuated for further medical evaluation on 17 July 2025
Third personnel evacuated for further medical evaluation on 18 July 2025
Failed hose submitted to laboratory for forensic analysis
BSEE conducted onsite investigation on 21 July 2025
event.summary: On 17 July 2025 at 0752 hours, during workover operations at Garden Banks Block 215, a 0.375-inch high-pressure hose ruptured while blowing down a coil tubing reel with nitrogen. The ruptured hose struck a Noble Assistant Driller on his left arm and an Oil States employee on the left side of his face. A Schlumberger Operator injured his knee while moving to avoid being struck. All three injuries were classified as First Aid Only. Laboratory analysis determined the hose failed due to chlorides corrosion coupled with rapid oxidation of wire reinforcement strands. The 3000 psi rated hose had only been in use for approximately 6 months and was pressurized to 2400 psi when it ruptured. Investigation found no safety restraints were installed on the manifold as recommended in Oil States Standard Operating Procedure, and there was no established hose inspection procedure or preventative maintenance program in place.
key_phrases: rapid oxidation of wire reinforcement strands
no safety restraints connected to the manifold

hazards.name: Pressurized nitrogen in coil tubing system

threats.name: Rapid oxidation of wire reinforcement strands

controls.name: Hose pressure rating design margin
controls.barrier_role: Prevent hose rupture under normal operating conditions
controls.lod_basis: Primary design control to prevent failure

supporting_text: the 3000 per square inch (psi) pressure rated hose was only in use for approximately 6 months and it was only pressurized up to 2400 psi when it ruptured
the hose ruptured due to chlorides corrosion coupled with rapid oxidation of wire reinforcement strands
there was no established hose inspection procedure or maintenance program in place
Inadequate equipment inspection. There is no formal hose inspection procedure in place

controls.name: Preventative maintenance program for hoses
controls.barrier_role: Maintain hose integrity through scheduled replacement and maintenance

supporting_text: There was no preventative maintenance program to evaluate the hose's operational condition
there was no established hose inspection procedure or maintenance program in place

controls.name: Safety restraints and whip checks on high-pressure hoses
controls.barrier_role: Prevent hose whipping and striking personnel upon rupture
controls.lod_basis: Secondary protective barrier to mitigate consequences

supporting_text: there being no safety restraints connected to the manifold as recommended in Oil States Standard Operation Procedure for Flow Iron and Restraint Installation
Not aware of hazards. There were no safety restraints or whip checks installed on the high-pressure hose as recommended in Oil States Standard Operation Procedure
when the 0.375-inch high-pressure hose ruptured, it struck and injured two of the three employees

controls.name: Personnel positioning and exclusion zones
controls.barrier_role: Keep personnel clear of potential hose whip zones

supporting_text: a 0.375-inch-high pressure hose ruptured, struck and injured a Noble Assistant Driller on his left arm and an Oil States employee on the left side of his face
a Schlumberger (SLB) Operator injured his knee when moving to avoid being struck by the hose

controls.name: Rig Medic evaluation and medical response
controls.barrier_role: Provide immediate medical assessment and treatment

supporting_text: All personnel reported to the Rig Medic for evaluation
After the medical evaluations, the decision was made to send the Noble Assistant Driller and SLB Operator in for further evaluation
All parties received a full return to work release, and this incident was classified as First Aid Only

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
